query: US spot Bitcoin ETF net flows late June 2026 and early July 2026, weekly trend from Farside Investors and SoSoValue. Are net outflows continuing into the week of June 29-July 3 2026? What is the Bitcoin price trend in late June 2026?
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's the bottom line:

**Are net outflows continuing into the week of June 29–July 3, 2026? Yes, clearly.**
- June 29: **-$231M**, June 30: **-$222.6M** — reaching **9 consecutive daily outflows** (SoSoValue).
- July 1 flows still reported as negative/"pressured" despite a brief price bounce.
- This follows the worst month on record: **~$4.5B June outflows** (final SoSoValue total; ~$4.06B in earlier reporting), beating the Feb 2025 record of ~$3.5B. IBIT drove ~79% (~$3.55B). The week of June 22–26 alone lost ~$1.79B (2nd/3rd-highest weekly ever), extending a 7+ week outflow streak.

**Bitcoin price trend in late June 2026: decisively bearish.**
- BTC fell ~**19–20.5% in June**, its worst month since June 2022.
- Traded **$58,000–$60,000**, hitting a two-year intraday low near **$57,700–$58,200** (lowest since ~Sept 2024), ~**53% below** the Oct 2025 ATH of ~$126,000.
- A brief July 1 bounce to ~$60,336 (+3.1%) on dovish Fed comments lacked institutional support, with $60K flipping from support to resistance.

Key caveat noted in the report: the June monthly outflow figure varies by source vintage ($4.06B early-reporting vs. $4.5B final-of-month). All flow data traces to SoSoValue and Farside Investors trackers as requested. Full synthesis, sources, and 24 structured data points have been recorded.

key_facts: US spot Bitcoin ETF outflows are continuing into the week of June 29-July 3, 2026: June 29 saw -$231M and June 30 saw -$222.6M, marking 9 consecutive daily outflows (SoSoValue).
June 2026 was the worst month ever for US spot Bitcoin ETFs: ~$4.06B-$4.5B net outflows (final total ~$4.5B), beating the Feb 2025 record of ~$3.48-3.56B.
BlackRock's IBIT accounted for ~79% (~$3.55B) of June outflows per Farside/SoSoValue.
Week ending June 26 (June 22-26): ~$1.79B net outflow, the second/third-highest weekly outflow on record; worst single day was June 25 at ~$696M.
The prior week saw ~$1.35B outflows, part of 7+ consecutive weeks of net outflows.
2026 YTD net outflows total roughly $5.0-5.5B; cumulative net inflows since Jan 2024 launch fell to ~$51.2B.
Total US spot BTC ETF net assets dropped to ~$70.9-73.2B, down from >$110B at year-start highs.
ETF BTC holdings fell below 1.25 million BTC, now lower than the same day a year earlier (CryptoQuant); ETFs sold ~71,600 BTC in June.
Bitcoin fell ~19-20.5% in June 2026, its worst month since June 2022 (-37%), closing as a near-wickless red 'Marubozu' candle.
Bitcoin traded $58,000-$60,000 in late June, hitting a two-year intraday low near $57,700-$58,200 (lowest since ~Sept 2024), ~53% below its Oct 2025 ATH of ~$126,000.
Fear & Greed Index dropped to 13 ('extreme fear', lowest since March 2020) on June 26.
July 1 brief bounce to ~$60,336 (+3.1%) on dovish Fed comments, but rally lacked institutional support and $60K flipped to resistance.
Citigroup on July 1 cut its 12-month BTC target to $82,000 from $112,000 and reduced its 12-month ETF inflow assumption to zero.
Intra-crypto divergence: XRP ETFs (+$59.5M) and HYPE ETFs (+$161M) saw June inflows while BTC, ETH (-$529M), and SOL saw outflows.
Bitcoin derivatives open interest fell from ~$31.3B (late May) to ~$21.6B, reducing violent-liquidation risk per Santiment/CryptoQuant.
